title: dianthus subscabridus
intro: Dianthus subscabridus, also known as the fringed pink or pink-fringed pink, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. Dianthus subscabridus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
